vocabulary.word: pitch / track / court / course / ring / rink

phrasal_verbs.meaning: change the date/time of an event so it happens earlier
defeat and remove from a competition; make unconscious
start (after planning to do sth for a long time)
stop being involved in an activity
do; do sth you should not do
start (a hobby, sport, etc); fill an amount of space/time

A.title: Topic vocabulary in contrast
A.instructions: Complete using the correct form of the words in the box.

questions.sentence: My friends and I love going to the local ice ...... on Friday evenings.
The golf ...... by the lake is considered one of the finest in the region.
The tennis ...... was still wet after the rain, so the match had to be postponed.
The young boxer stepped into the ...... for his first professional fight.
Runners must stay in their lane when competing on the athletics ......
The groundkeepers worked hard to keep the football ...... in perfect condition.
Our team ...... for most of the first half, but the opponents ...... twice in extra time, and so they ...... us in the end. What a game!
You need a strong fishing ...... when you're trying to catch large fish in the river.
My tennis ...... broke during the final set, which was really frustrating.
Ice hockey ...... are made of composite materials to make them light but strong.
She bought a new cricket ...... just before the summer season began.

B.title: Circle the correct word
B.instructions: Circle the correct word or phrase.

questions.sentence: The **umpire / referee** stopped play immediately and issued the defender a yellow card.
During the summer term we focused on **athletics / sport**, and I particularly enjoyed the hurdles.
Fancy a quick **game / play** of darts before we head home?
He has been cycling for years purely for fun and has never thought of becoming **amateur / a professional** racer.
The show was so tedious that half the audience had left by **time / the interval**.
Our local team **equalled / drew** 2-2 with the visitors in a thrilling match on Sunday.
**Spectators / Viewers** who watched last night's broadcast will have seen the dramatic photo finish to the race.
After three nail-biting rounds, our club finally made it to the **final / finale**.
I love board games that have a surprising **end / ending** that nobody sees coming.
Would all **opponents / competitors** please register at the main desk before the start of the event?

C.title: Phrasal verbs (transformation)
C.instructions: Complete each second sentence using the word given, so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence. Write between two and five words in each gap.

questions.sentence:
I don't know how you can stand getting changed in a crowded locker room before training. **put**
I don't know how you can ...... changed in a crowded locker room before training.

I've finally started sorting out my collection of football programmes. **round**
I've finally ...... sorting out my collection of football programmes.

What did you do at the sports centre last weekend? **get**
What did you ...... at the sports centre last weekend?

I used to love skiing, but now I prefer snowboarding. **gone**
I've ...... since I took up snowboarding.

Why do you continue to have swimming lessons if you're afraid of the water? **on**
Why do you ...... swimming lessons if you're afraid of the water?

We simply can't delay the match any longer — we need to play it this week. **put**
We simply can't ...... the match any longer.

D.title: Phrasal verbs (gap fill)
D.instructions: Write one word in each gap.

questions.sentence: My younger sister never wanted to join ...... when we played football in the garden.
Look ...... for that pothole on the running path — it's deeper than it looks!
Since joining the cycling club, Dan's ...... to warming up properly before every ride.
She was thinking of taking ...... rock climbing until she found out how expensive the gear is.
They were knocked ...... of the cup in the quarterfinal after a penalty shootout.
Could we bring the training session ...... to Monday rather than leaving it until next week?
He had to pull ...... of the relay race after pulling a muscle in his thigh.
My brother doesn't ...... in for competitive sports; he much prefers hiking.
Come on, ...... a go at the climbing wall — it's much easier than it looks!
He would have made a brilliant ski jumper if he weren't so afraid of .......
There's very little ...... of us qualifying for the national finals this season.
My neighbour is absolutely mad ...... cycling and goes out before dawn every morning.
Just ...... your best and enjoy the game — that's all anyone can ask of you.
It's ...... time you learned the rules properly if you want to join the team.
My grandfather ...... a great deal of pleasure from watching cricket on television every summer.
...... your time lacing up your boots — rushing can cause twisted ankles.
We take it ...... turn to choose which board game we play on Friday evenings.
Time ...... so quickly when you're fully absorbed in a chess match.
The new swimming coach is very popular ...... all the children at the leisure centre.
As a child, she'd ...... whole afternoons playing tennis against the garden wall.
He had been planning to leave the squad the ...... time, without telling a soul.
I'm not siding ...... her just because we're teammates, but because she's right.
My daughter has never been very fond **D** of playing in goal.
The new midfielder is really interested **A** in playing for the school team.
questions.answer: A – in playing for the school team.

questions.sentence: If you want to improve quickly, you need to concentrate **G** on getting fit.
As a parent, I always found it difficult **B** to find enough time to have any hobbies.
questions.answer: B – to find enough time to have any hobbies.

questions.sentence: Before we decide on tactics, let's listen **F** to what the coach thinks first.
questions.answer: F – to what the coach thinks first.

questions.sentence: After the third hole, we stopped **C** to try to find the golf balls we'd lost.
questions.answer: C – to try to find the golf balls we'd lost.

questions.sentence: A sudden downpour stopped us **E** from finishing the game.

G.title: Cloze (Word patterns)
G.instructions: Water has damaged part of this text. Read it and decide what you think each of the original words was. Write the words in the blank spaces.

questions.sentence: When I first tried archery at my local sports club, I ...... the whole experience surprisingly challenging.
Even so, I was eager to get involved ...... competitions as quickly as possible.
I loved the idea of testing myself ...... other archers from across the region,
but joining a formal league ...... travelling to different venues every single weekend,
and ...... meant spending long days away from home.
It was not easy ...... squeeze training sessions into my already busy schedule,
but my coach always ...... sure that I practised my technique at least three times a week.
Even so, I felt I ...... always one step behind the more experienced archers.
I tried to ...... everything they suggested and never missed a session,
...... than give up when things got tough.

H.title: Word formation (sentences)
H.instructions: Complete the sentences by changing the form of the word in capitals when this is necessary.

questions.sentence: I wore my old ...... (TRAIN) to the park, which was a mistake as they fell apart halfway round the track.
The coach told everyone to ...... (PRACTICE) their penalty kicks every day before the main training session.
It was the most ...... (INTEREST) documentary about extreme sports I had ever watched.
She is a highly ...... (COMPETE) player who always pushes herself to be the best.
Some top-flight footballers earn a small ...... (FORTUNE) each week in wages and sponsorships.
W.T.A. stands for Women's Tennis ...... (ASSOCIATE).
The gold, silver and bronze ...... (MEDAL) gathered on the podium to receive their awards.
His weekly ...... (ALLOW) was just enough to cover the cost of his swimming lessons.
The gym will be closed this week for essential ...... (MAINTAIN) work on the pool pumps.

I.title: Word formation (text)
I.instructions: Use the word given in capitals at the end of each line to form a word that fits in the gap in the same line.

questions.sentence: When I first started playing table tennis, I had very little (1) ...... (KNOW) of how to hold the bat correctly.
I simply thought it looked like an (2) ...... (ENJOY) way to spend an afternoon with friends.
Luckily, the sports centre provided all the (3) ...... (EQUIP) I needed, so I didn't have to spend any money at first.
I spent (4) ...... (PRACTICAL) every evening after school in the hall practising, and eventually decided to enter a local (5) ...... (COMPETE).
My (6) ...... (OPPOSE) in the first round had been playing since she was six years old.
She won comfortably, but the fact that I had (7) ...... (LOSE) didn't discourage me in the slightest.
And, (8) ...... (FORTUNE), my game improved so much that I won my first club trophy just one year later.
